name: Zucchini Frittata
recipeIngredient: 2 Large Swiss Chard leaves (including stems), coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
A frittata is a flat omelet with a mixture of vegetables and herbs. This recipe calls for zucchini and chard, but you can substitute any summer squash for zucchini, and spinach for the chard.
1. Heat oil in a wide frying pan over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, chard, and zucchini; cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are soft (about 5 minutes). Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
2. Beat eggs lightly with pepper, basil, and oregano. Stir in cheese and vegetables.
3. Pour into a greased 9-inch pie pan. Bake in a 350 degree oven for 25 to 30 minutes or until puffed and brown. Serve hot or at room temperature.
